title: Pilosella latisquamiformis
taxonomy: Pilosella latisquamiformis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Pilosella. The genus Pilosella is closely related to the genus Hieracium and is often considered a subgroup of it. However, Pilosella is sometimes treated as a distinct genus. The classification of Pilosella latisquamiformis may vary depending on the taxonomic system used.

intro: Pilosella latisquamiformis, also known as the woolly hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella latisquamiformis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pilosella latisquamiformis is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive problems and skin issues.
